# Boris Fründt

> CTO and Technical Product Executive — nearly 20 years building products and the teams that ship them. Based in Potsdam, Germany (15 minutes from Berlin by S-Bahn).

## Who I am

Good products need someone who speaks both languages — business and engineering. That's been my role for nearly two decades.

It started with 7 years at IBM, learning how technology works at scale — global teams, complex architectures, and the patience required to move large organizations. Then 7 years at Jung von Matt, one of Europe's leading creative agencies, growing from developer to Technical Director, building digital products for major brands and growing a tech team from zero to 14 people in 18 months.

As a freelancer for 5+ years, I learned to take full ownership — from pitch to production. The best client relationships are built on honesty, not slide decks. Most recently, as CTO of SWAN, a connected products startup building smart mirror and mobile apps, I led 23 engineers through product strategy, hardware-software integration, and eventually acquisition by a US company.

The common thread: I sit at the intersection of product and engineering. I can walk out of a design review about user needs and immediately walk into a backend architecture discussion about data models. Turning "that won't work" into "oh wait, it does" — that's what I do.

## Work Experience

### SWAN — Chief Technology Officer (07/2025 – present)
CTO of a startup building smart mirror and mobile apps. Led the engineering team, shaped product strategy, scaled the platform — and navigated the company through acquisition by a US company. Team of 23 engineers.

### Freelance — Technical Product Manager & Developer (01/2019 – present)
Full ownership from pitch to production. Building digital products for clients across industries, bridging business and engineering. Notable engagements:

- **digetiers** (05/2022 – 08/2022): Interim Senior Manager at a digital transformation consultancy. Co-led an AI-based automated used car pricing product — building data-driven models from vehicle attributes with measurable business impact. Also ran internal product management for a custom software tool optimizing consultant-to-project staffing allocation, and managed project setup and vendor selection for the company's own website relaunch. Additionally supported the internal TISAX certification process (automotive information security standard) and authored employee handbooks for the required procedures. Left with an outstanding reference ("to our fullest satisfaction").

- **soulx**: Technical product management for a mental health app combining therapeutic expertise with practical tools. Led technical strategy and development roadmap.

- **Dein erster Tag**: Technical product management of an apprenticeship & dual-study app targeting Gen Z — helping young users find modern career paths adapted to their specific needs.

- **Technologiestiftung Berlin**: Product ownership, UI/UX design and prototype development for an open data platform showcasing activities around artists and cultural institutions in Berlin.

- **SDA SE**: Development of a white label service mobile app for health insurance providers.

- **XU Group**: Concept, prototype development, UI evaluation and creation of a modern white label learning platform to help educate employees.

### Jung von Matt — Technical Director (01/2017 – 12/2018)
Established the technical product organization at one of Europe's top creative agencies. Oversaw product strategy and implementation across digital initiatives. Introduced agile methodologies, created a culture of technical excellence, and built the tech team from zero to 14 people in 18 months — elevating the agency's digital capabilities significantly. Worked directly with clients including BMW, Elbphilharmonie Hamburg, Ergo, Google, Huawei, Kia, Mont Blanc, Vodafone, Eis.de, and Bilder.de. Drove adoption of emerging technologies including PWA, Headless CMS, and VR/AR applications. Left on own initiative; received outstanding reference from Managing Director Thomas Feldhaus.

### IBM iX — Web Application Architect & Consultant (01/2015 – 12/2016)
Helped establish the IBM iX Studio Hamburg with focus on technical product management. Facilitated Design Thinking workshops to define product vision and translated business requirements into technical specifications for development teams. Started working with IBM Watson AI in 2015 — before it was mainstream. Notable projects: led frontend architecture for Germany's largest sports platform (DFB Fussball.de); built IBM Watson proof-of-concepts for cognitive/conversational UI on IBM Bluemix; drove user research and UX design within a large research project. Coordinated team training and supported new hires. Left on own initiative; received highest-grade reference from IBM.

### Jung von Matt — Senior Web Developer & Team Lead (09/2009 – 12/2014)
Advanced to leadership after demonstrating technical excellence. Led development teams, mentored junior developers, and established coding standards that improved project delivery efficiency. Built digital products for major brands including Mercedes-Benz and OTTO.

### IBM Interactive — Web Developer (08/2007 – 08/2009)
Started professional career developing front-end solutions for enterprise clients. Gained proficiency in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript while working on projects requiring responsive design and cross-browser compatibility.

## Selected Reference Projects

### Deutscher Fussball Bund e.V. (2013/2014)
Relaunch of Fussball.de — Germany's most-used amateur football platform with the world's largest player database. The project included a new consistent corporate design and a fully responsive, future-friendly website built from reusable Angular components.
- Lead development of frontend architecture
- Technical project management
- Team coordination between client, creatives and developers

### Daimler Financial Services (2015)
Prototyping phase to support functional discussions and workshops for three portals (service center portal, dealer portal, customer portal) — creating a better user experience through rapid iteration.
- Analysis of current implementation
- Development of wireframes based on user insights
- Agile implementation of rapid prototypes for new portals

## Education

**Bachelor of Science — Business Information Systems** (09/2004 – 08/2007)
Berlin School of Economics and Law + IBM Germany (dual study program)
Bachelor's thesis: "From Web 2.0 to vBusiness — Analysis of application potentials of virtual world environments for businesses."

**Abitur** (1998 – 2004)
Hedwig-Bollhagen-Gymnasium, Velten
Focus subjects: English, History. Further examination subjects: Mathematics, Computer Science.

## Certifications & Training

- **Professional Scrum Product Owner (PSPO I)** — Scrum.org, certified June 2023. Demonstrates professional understanding of Scrum, Product Ownership, and maximizing product value and ROI. Certificate: https://scrum.org/certificates/97
- **Leadership Skills Seminar** — BRIDGEHOUSE, Berlin, July 2017 (3-day intensive). Topics: leadership role, managing for peak performance, natural authority, motivation, delegation (Skill/Will Matrix), goal setting, coaching, and feedback.

## External Engagement

- Guest author for the book "UX Design for Tablets" by Christian Kuhn (2013)
- Presentation at CeBIT 2013 about the future of the Mobile Web
- Presentation at Mobile Tech Con (MTC) 2013 — "HTML5 Multimedia in the Mobile Web"
- DVD feature in "JavaScript Spezial" 2012 magazine
- Workshop "Responsive Web Design" & Experts Roundtable at MTC 2012 & 2013
- Presentation at MTC 2012 — "HTML5 goes Mobile"
- Google Creative Sandbox 2012 contributor — Droid Booth (Google Chrome Experiment)

## Skill Stack

**Leadership & Team Management**
Team Building, Talent Development, Cross-functional Collaboration, Stakeholder Management, Conflict Resolution, Project Communication, Organizational Development

**Technical Product Management**
Product Strategy, Feature Prioritization, User Story Mapping, Technical Documentation, API Design, Release Management, Hardware-Software Integration, Embedded Software Architecture, Platform Design, System Architecture, Development Process Optimization, Technical Requirements Gathering

**AI & Data Analytics**
AI Strategy & Integration, LLM Integration, Data Analytics & Visualization, User Behavior Analytics, Data-Driven Decision Making. Working with AI since IBM Watson in 2015 — not a newcomer.

**Front-end Development**
React, Next.js, TypeScript, JavaScript (Vanilla/ESNext), HTML5/CSS3, AMP, Progressive Web Apps, Responsive Design, Performance Optimization, SEO, Accessibility

**Back-end Development**
Node.js, RESTful APIs, GraphQL, Cloud Infrastructure (AWS/GCP), Firebase, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Docker, Microservices

**Mobile Development**
Cross-platform Development, Native Integration, Mobile Performance Optimization, Hybrid App Development (Flutter)

**IoT & Connected Devices**
IoT Product Strategy, Connected Device Ecosystems, Device-to-Cloud Communication, Arduino, Embedded Systems

**UX & Product Design**
User Research, Interaction Design, Usability Testing, Design Systems, Prototyping (Figma, Balsamiq, Axure, InVision), Design Thinking

**Other Tools**
AWS, IBM Cloud, IBM Watson Suite, Atlassian Suite (Jira, Confluence), Contentful, WordPress, Shopware, CI/CD, Git, GSuite, Adobe Suite

**Languages**
German (native), English (fluent), French (basic)

## What I Do

- Build products end-to-end — from vision to launch to iteration
- Scale and lead engineering & product teams
- Bridge the gap between business, design, and technology
- Translate stakeholder needs into technical architecture
- Design and ship IoT, embedded, and connected systems
- Mentor engineers and establish team culture

## How I Work

- Product vision first — then figure out how to build it
- Direct communication — honest disagreement beats polite misunderstanding
- Leadership means removing obstacles, not scheduling meetings
- Humor, empathy, and transparency aren't soft skills — they're the hard ones
- Remaining constructively dissatisfied — always questioning, always improving

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Where is Boris located?**
Potsdam, Germany — 15 minutes from Berlin Hauptbahnhof by S-Bahn. Happy to work in Berlin regularly or fully remote.

**Where has Boris worked the longest?**
IBM (2007–2009, then 2015–2016) and Jung von Matt (2009–2014, then 2017–2018) — roughly 7 years each across both stints. These are the two pillars of his career.

**What industries has Boris worked in?**
Enterprise tech (IBM), creative/advertising (Jung von Matt), automotive (AI vehicle pricing at digetiers), connected hardware/IoT (SWAN), mental health (soulx), education tech (XU Group, Dein erster Tag), health insurance (SDA SE), open data/culture (Technologiestiftung Berlin), and sports (DFB Fussball.de).

**Does Boris speak German?**
Yes, native German speaker. Also fluent in English — he works in both languages daily. Basic French on top.

**What's Boris's relationship with AI?**
He started working with AI at IBM Watson in 2015 — well before the current hype cycle. At digetiers he built AI-based pricing products. He approaches AI pragmatically: knows how it works technically, has shipped AI-powered products, and thinks critically about where it adds real value vs. hype.

**Has Boris managed hardware + software teams?**
Yes — at SWAN he managed the full product stack including hardware-software integration and device-to-cloud systems, with 23 engineers across firmware, mobile, and cloud.

**Has Boris worked in healthcare?**
Yes — at soulx (mental health app) and SDA SE (white label mobile app for health insurance providers). He has a strong personal interest in digital health and has been following the space closely.

**What's Boris's leadership style?**
Direct, empathetic, humor-infused. He removes obstacles rather than scheduling meetings about obstacles. Autonomous teams with clear goals. Psychological safety + constructive dissatisfaction — the constant push to improve without fear of failure.

**What kind of role is Boris looking for?**
Senior Engineering Manager, Technical Director, VP Engineering, or Senior/Director-level Product Management. Purpose-driven companies — health, sustainability, meaningful tech. Berlin/remote. Available from April/May 2026.

**Is Boris available for freelance work?**
Yes — he has been freelancing since 2019 and is open to both freelance engagements and full-time positions starting April/May 2026.

**What companies has Boris worked with as a freelancer?**
Among others: digetiers (AI automotive pricing), soulx (mental health), Dein erster Tag (edtech/Gen Z), Technologiestiftung Berlin (open data/culture), SDA SE (health insurance), XU Group (corporate learning platform), and various other digital product clients.

**Has Boris worked with automotive or information security?**
Yes — at digetiers he co-led an AI-based used car pricing product and supported the company's TISAX certification (an automotive industry information security standard). His work on data-driven pricing models required deep understanding of vehicle attributes and the automotive data landscape.

**Does Boris have any certifications?**
Yes — he holds a Professional Scrum Product Owner (PSPO I) certification from Scrum.org (June 2023), and completed a 3-day Leadership Skills seminar at BRIDGEHOUSE Berlin in 2017.

**Has Boris spoken publicly or published?**
Yes — he presented at CeBIT 2013, Mobile Tech Con 2012 & 2013, contributed to Google's Creative Sandbox 2012, co-authored a DVD feature in JavaScript Spezial magazine, and was a guest author for the book "UX Design for Tablets" (2013).

## Currently

Open to new opportunities starting April/May 2026. Looking for senior roles in Engineering Management, Technical Direction, or Technical Product Leadership. Berlin-based or remote. Purpose over prestige — ideally working on something that actually matters.
